{"problem_standardized_math":"Let \\(f(x)=x^TQx\\), where \\(Q\\) is an \\(n\\times n\\) symmetric positive semidefinite matrix. Using the definition of convexity\n\\[\nf(\\alpha x+(1-\\alpha)y)\\le \\alpha f(x)+(1-\\alpha)f(y),\\qquad \\text{for all } \\alpha\\in[0,1],\n\\]\nshow that \\(f\\) is convex on \\(\\mathbf{R}^n\\). As a hint, it may be convenient to prove the equivalent inequality\n\\[\nf\\bigl(y+\\alpha(x-y)\\bigr)-\\alpha f(x)-(1-\\alpha)f(y)\\le 0,\n\\]\nfor all \\(\\alpha\\in[0,1]\\) and all \\(x,y\\in\\mathbf{R}^n\\)."}
